stem: A bike shop rents bicycles to customers. The shop earns revenue of $18 for each bicycle rented for the day. On Saturday, the shop earned $480 in profit from renting bicycles after paying daily expenses of $96. How many bicycles did the shop rent on Saturday? (profit = total revenue − total expenses)
rationale: Let n be the number of bicycles rented. Revenue is 18n, so profit = 18n - 96 = 480. Then 18n = 576 and n = 32.
